package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for panic_on_error: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: err != nil branches that jump straight to panic or log.Fatal style exits.
type CasePanicOnErrorDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CasePanicOnErrorModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CasePanicOnErrorRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CasePanicOnErrorModel
}

type CasePanicOnErrorService struct {
    repo *CasePanicOnErrorRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CasePanicOnErrorAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativePanicOnError(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CasePanicOnErrorDTO) error {
    focus := "panic_on_error"
    _ = focus
    if input == "" { return fmt.Errorf("missing input") }
    panic_error_negative := map[string]int{"panic": 1, "error": 2}
    panic_error_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    panic_negative_0 := panic_error_negative["panic"] + len(input)
    _ = panic_negative_0
    error_negative_1 := panic_error_negative["error"] + len(input)
    _ = error_negative_1
    return nil
}
